entries.d: To have a big argument and stop being friendly
entries.s: I _____ with my best friend Anna last week because she told everyone my private secret at school.

entries.d: To leave a room quickly because you are angry
entries.s: He was so angry that he _____ out of the classroom, slamming the door loudly behind him.

entries.d: To end a romantic connection
entries.s: She decided to _____ with her boyfriend of two years after a big argument last weekend.

entries.g: sich um seine eigenen Angelegenheiten kümmern
entries.d: To not ask about things that are not about you
entries.s: Stop asking me so many personal questions about my family and _____ your own business!

entries.d: To become friends again after an argument
entries.s: They had a big fight yesterday morning, but today they _____ up and are best friends again.

entries.w: to get on well with sb.
entries.d: To have a good, friendly connection
entries.s: I _____ on really well with my older cousin Sarah — we always laugh together and never argue about anything.

entries.d: A way to say that something is not your problem
entries.s: Who she chooses to talk to at break time is _____. I should not get involved or ask questions.

entries.d: To make fun of someone by laughing
entries.s: The mean children in the playground pointed and _____ him when he tripped over his shoelace and fell on the ground.

entries.d: To decide something after thinking about it
entries.s: I can't _____ my mind — should I buy the blue jumper or the red one for my sister's birthday present?

entries.d: To say or do things to laugh at someone in a mean way
entries.s: Don't _____ of him just because he's new at school and doesn't know anyone yet — that's mean and unkind.

entries.d: To go and live in a different place
entries.s: We're going to _____ to a much bigger new flat next month in a different neighbourhood across town.
mc: to stay in our current flat

entries.d: A cuddly animal made of fabric and stuffing
entries.s: She sleeps every night with her favourite _____ — a fluffy stuffed elephant made of very soft brown fabric.

entries.d: To help or do something when there is a problem
entries.s: When the two angry boys started pushing each other in the playground, the teacher had to _____ and stop the fight.

entries.d: The way two people feel about and behave towards each other
entries.s: A good, healthy _____ with your close friends based on trust and respect is very important in life.

col.prompt: Which does NOT go with RELATIONSHIP?

entries.d: To have something that is yours
entries.s: Does your family _____ a car, or do you rent one every time you need to drive somewhere?

entries.d: The time when you are a child
entries.s: My happiest _____ memories are from summer holidays swimming at the lake with my cousins when I was 7 or 8.

entries.d: A piece of jewellery you wear on your ear
entries.s: She lost one of her favourite gold _____ at the swimming pool — it must have fallen out of her ear in the water.

entries.d: Wanting what someone else has, or not wanting to share
entries.s: She was very _____ because her classmate got a better grade on the test than she did, even though she studied harder.

entries.d: To not tell anyone about something private
entries.s: Can you _____ a secret? I have something important and personal to tell you — please don't tell anyone else.

entries.d: A list of questions you answer, often on paper
entries.s: Please fill in this _____ form with 20 questions about your reading habits and favourite types of books.

entries.d: To speak to someone angrily because they did something wrong
entries.s: My strict mum _____ me off for coming home too late from my friend's house last Friday night — I was an hour late.

entries.d: To find the answer to a problem
entries.s: Can you please help me _____ this tricky maths word problem? I've been stuck on it for 30 minutes already.

entries.d: Loved very much
entries.s: The kind old woman sat peacefully with her _____ old cat Whiskers on her lap, gently stroking its soft fur.

entries.d: Not in any place, not anywhere
entries.s: There was _____ to sit because all twenty seats on the train were already taken by other passengers.

entries.d: The written text of a film or play
entries.s: The film actors all read the _____ together around a table before they started filming the scenes with cameras.

entries.d: To try very hard to do something that is difficult
entries.s: He _____ a lot with his English homework because it was very hard and he didn't understand the new grammar rules.

entries.d: To say something that is not true on purpose
entries.s: She _____ her teacher about why she was late to school this morning — she said the bus was late but it wasn't true.
mc: to tell the truth to sb.
to give correct information to sb.

entries.d: To say that something is true, especially something bad
entries.s: He didn't want to _____ that he broke the classroom window with his football, even though everyone saw him do it.

entries.d: To force someone to do what you want by saying you will tell a secret
entries.s: He _____ his classmate by saying he would tell the whole class her embarrassing secret unless she gave him €10.

entries.d: Often dropping things or falling over by accident
entries.s: I'm so _____ today — I've already knocked over my glass of water twice at the lunch table without meaning to.

entries.d: Always telling the truth
entries.s: Please be _____ with me and tell me the truth — did you really like my drawing or were you just being polite?

entries.d: A lot of things put on top of each other
entries.s: There is _____ dirty, smelly clothes stacked high on the floor of his messy bedroom that he hasn't washed for weeks.

entries.d: Red spots on your skin, often because of an allergy
entries.s: She got an itchy red _____ with bumps all over her arms and neck after touching the strange poisonous plant in the forest.

entries.d: Not feeling well, a bit ill
entries.s: He looked very pale and _____, so the school teacher sent him home to rest in bed with some hot tea.
